steps.title: Test the first nonmatching system with intercepts
steps.content: The graph in the question gives three fast targets: both lines share the $x$-intercept $(8,0)$, and the $y$-intercepts are positive, about $(0,3.2)$ and $(0,6.4)$. The first nonmatching system has lines that hit the $x$-axis at $(4,0)$ and $(6.4,0)$, so they do not share the required intercept $(8,0)$.

steps.title: Test another nonmatching system with the same targets
steps.content: The same intercept check fails because the two lines cross the $x$-axis at $(4,0)$ and $(6.4,0)$ instead of sharing $(8,0)$. They also slope upward from left to right, while the lines in the question slope downward.

steps.title: Test the system with the correct x-intercept but wrong y-intercepts
steps.content: The system in C passes the shared-intercept check: both lines go through $(8,0)$. But the $y$-intercepts are on the wrong side of the axis: $(0,-3.2)$ and $(0,-6.4)$ instead of positive $(0,3.2)$ and $(0,6.4)$. That wrong vertical placement eliminates C.

steps.title: Test the correct answer last
steps.content: The expression matches the graph. The first line, $4x+10y=32$, passes through $(8,0)$ and $(0,3.2)$. The second line, $-8x-10y=-64$, passes through $(8,0)$ and $(0,6.4)$. Both lines slope downward, so D matches the shared $x$-intercept, both positive $y$-intercepts, and the direction of the graph.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: A and B miss the shared $x$-intercept. C has the correct shared $x$-intercept but the wrong side of the $x$-axis. D is the only pair with shared $x$-intercept $(8,0)$, positive $y$-intercepts $(0,3.2)$ and $(0,6.4)$, and downward slopes. D is correct.
